title: Justicia jujuyensis
taxonomy: Justicia jujuyensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae. It is native to Argentina. The plant is classified in the genus Justicia, which comprises about 600 species of herbs, shrubs, and trees. Justicia jujuyensis is specifically placed within the Acanthaceae family, which includes approximately 2,684 species of flowering plants. The classification of Justicia jujuyensis reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants within these taxonomic groups.

intro: Justicia jujuyensis, also known as Quebrachillo, is a plant species in the Acanthaceae family, native to the Andean region of Argentina and Bolivia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, making it effective in treating various health conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Justicia jujuyensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and diterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments in traditional medicine.
